dossier.overview:
**Pokémon memory system** is the durable history layer for each Resort Pokémon (`pkrid`). It stores memory **tokens**, not a raw event log.

**Two different feeling systems:**

1. **Cartridge memories (Gen 6+)** use a stored **feeling index** per memory. The game does **not** infer feeling from friendship, Nature, species, or battle outcome later. Each `memoryId` has a **whitelist bitmask** of legal feelings; the index is chosen when the memory is created and then stored (OT Memory Feeling / NotOT Memory Feeling fields).

2. **Resort-authored memories** (island activities, staff moments, resort-recorded transfer intake) use a **Resort feeling resolver**: activity + map context + **typing + Nature** (example: Fire type, Careful Nature, near the pool → nervous).

We also preserve **Characteristic-style memory text** (OT / current trainer strings), **transfer hop records**, **game-context deltas** (evolve, ribbon, move), and **silent cartridge snapshots** on cache miss when Resort opens.

sections.title: Memory families
sections.summary: Four sources, one store per pkrid.

blocks.title: Memory sources
blocks.caption: All families merge into PokemonMemoryRegistry; actors read, never own
blocks.source:
flowchart TB
    GN["cartridge memoryId intensity feeling variable"] --> REG["PokemonMemoryRegistry per pkrid"]
    TR["transfer hop records"] --> REG
    RS["resort memories"] --> RFR["ResortFeelingResolver type nature activity"]
    RFR --> REG
    GC["game_context and cache snapshots"] --> REG
    REG --> SURF["Surprise flavor at resort arrival or interact"]
    REG --> NPC["NPC AI memoryScore bias"]

blocks.html:
FamilyFeeling modelExample
cartridgeStored feelingRaw + per-memoryId bitmask (Gen 6/8 rules)Memory 63, intensity 2, feeling 9 → "it got nervous"
transferPreserve imported bytes; resort intake uses Resort resolverLink trade hop; first arrival at island
resortResortFeelingResolver (activity + type + Nature + map)Fire + Careful + pool edge → nervous
game_contextContext fields; optional mapped feeling for display onlyLavender Town party snapshot before badge 6

Guardrail: never re-roll cartridge feelings from personality later. Resort feelings are computed once at creation time.

blocks.caption: Unified memory record shape
blocks.body:
{
  "registryId": "mem_000482",
  "pkrid": "pkr_000123",
  "family": "resort",
  "memoryType": "played_ball_together",
  "createdAt": 1234567890.0,
  "importance": 65,
  "feelingSource": "resort_resolver",
  "feelingNormalized": 1,
  "renderedFeeling": "it had fun",
  "participants": ["pkr_000123", "pkr_000456", "player"],
  "mapId": "beach_01",
  "smartObjectId": "beach_ball_01",
  "displayHint": "The Pokémon looks excited near the beach ball."
}

sections.title: Cartridge memories and feelings
sections.summary: Stored feeling index per memoryId. Not re-derived from Nature or species later.

blocks.body:
A Gen 6+ cartridge memory is not a free-text mood. It is a tuple:

• **memoryId** (what happened)
• **intensity** (remembers / clearly remembers / fondly remembers)
• **feeling** (emotion line index)
• **variable** (place, item, move, species, etc.)

Separate OT vs current-trainer tracks: **OT Memory Feeling** and **NotOT Memory Feeling** sit beside memory line, intensity, and text variable fields.

**Do not** infer feeling from friendship, Nature, species, affection, or battle result when reading a save. Store the **raw feeling byte** for legality and Bank/HOME export.

blocks.caption: Normalize Gen 6 vs Gen 8 feeling storage

blocks.body:
When the **game** creates a memory, it picks one **legal** feeling for that `memoryId` from a 24-bit whitelist (PKHeX legality model). Resort must **not** invent cartridge feelings outside that mask when exporting back to a save.

**Special case:** Gen 6 memory `4` (Link Trade) with `variable === 0` (Bank-style "came through Link Trade") uses `rand(0,10)` and **does not** use the normal bit table.

blocks.caption: chooseFeeling (game-accurate sketch)

blocks.html:
Gen 6 indexGen 8 indexRendered text
01it was happy
12it had fun
23it was glad
34it grinned
45it got overwhelmed by emotion
56its feelings were indescribable / complicated
67it felt good
78it got teary eyed
89it got lighthearted
910it got nervous
1011it felt comfortable
1112it was restless
1213it got a bit carried away
1314it felt sorry
1415it got emotional
1516it felt nostalgic
1617it had some difficulty
1718it felt exhausted
1819it could not be true to its feelings
1920it felt proud
2021they ended up in a foul mood
2122it got angry
2223it got jealous
2324it got sleepy

Source: Bulbapedia memory/feeling indexes (Gen 6/Gen 8). Characteristic text ("Likes to relax") is a separate IV-derived field, not this feeling line.

sections.title: Transfer memories
sections.summary: Preserve imported cartridge bytes; resort intake uses Resort feeling resolver.

blocks.html:
Imported from cartridge: copy full OT/NotOT memory tuples unchanged (memoryId, intensity, feelingRaw, variable). Validate with per-memoryId feeling bitmask on write/export.

Resort transfer log (OpenHome hop, first island arrival): store hop metadata plus a Resort-resolved feeling (see Resort memories). Map display to nearest game feeling text where helpful; do not pretend it was a native cartridge roll unless exporting.

  • Left game A for game B (OpenHome)
  • First available at Resort from origin game
  • Returned to home region save after island visit (optional)

blocks.caption: Resort transfer log entry (feeling from Resort resolver)
blocks.body:
{
  "registryId": "mem_000201",
  "pkrid": "pkr_000123",
  "family": "transfer",
  "memoryType": "arrived_at_resort",
  "feelingSource": "resort_resolver",
  "feelingNormalized": 8,
  "renderedFeeling": "it got lighthearted",
  "fromGameId": "emerald",
  "regionHint": "hoenn",
  "importance": 70,
  "createdAt": 1234561000.0,
  "resolverInputs": {
    "activityId": "resort_intake",
    "primaryType": "Water",
    "nature": "Jolly",
    "mapTags": ["dock", "arrival"]
  },
  "displayHint": "It seems lighthearted since arriving from Hoenn."
}

blocks.title: Transfer to memory token
blocks.caption: Transfer pipeline writes; resort reads later
blocks.source:
sequenceDiagram
    participant G1 as Origin game
    participant OH as OpenHome transfer
    participant REG as MemoryRegistry
    participant RS as Resort intake
    G1->>OH: Pokemon sent to box or game B
    OH->>REG: transfer memory plus emotion
    OH->>RS: Pokemon available in Resort
    RS->>REG: arrived_at_resort memory
    Note over REG: Player meets Pokemon days later
    REG->>RS: displayHint for interact flavor

sections.title: Resort memories
sections.summary: Island events. Feelings from activity + type + Nature + map context.

blocks.body:
Only **Resort-authored** memories use dynamic feeling selection. Inputs:

• **activityId** / smart object tags (played ball, pool edge, woken late)
• **primaryType** / **secondaryType**
• **Nature** (and optionally Characteristic as light bias)
• **mapTags** (pool, water, quiet, night)
• **timeBlock** (late_night wake-ups)

Output: `feelingNormalized` (0 to 23) and `renderedFeeling` string aligned with the cartridge table for consistent UI. Optionally clamp to a resort-specific whitelist per `memoryType` if we export to cartridge later.

**Example:** Fire type, **Careful** Nature, standing near **pool** water → uncomfortable → **nervous** (index 9, "it got nervous"). Water type at same pool might resolve **comfortable** (index 10).

blocks.title: ResortFeelingResolver
blocks.caption: Cartridge memories skip this path entirely
blocks.source:
flowchart TD
    EVT["Resort event completed"] --> IN["Inputs activity types nature mapTags time"]
    IN --> RULES["Data table type nature zone rules"]
    RULES --> IDX["feelingNormalized 0..23"]
    IDX --> TXT["renderedFeeling string"]
    IDX --> REG["PokemonMemoryRegistry"]

blocks.caption: Example rule rows (data-driven)

blocks.html:
memoryTypeTrigger
played_ball_togetherSmart object activity completed with friend
napped_near_friendRest activity near bonded pkrid
made_staffPlayer assigned staff role on map
assigned_special_spacePlayer asked Pokémon to stay in rest nook, villa room, etc.
woken_late_nightPlayer interaction during late_night time block
received_photoPhoto gift linked as treasured anchor
player_joined_gamePlayer accepted multi-actor activity invite
worked_staff_shiftStaff work loop completed

blocks.body: Created by **activity completion hooks**, **player assignment**, and **staff role changes**. Feed NPC AI `memoryScore` and interact flavor. Photo gifts link `photoId` and `linkedRegistryId`.

blocks.caption: Pool-edge nervous Fire / Careful example
blocks.body:
{
  "registryId": "mem_000315",
  "pkrid": "pkr_000456",
  "family": "resort",
  "memoryType": "pool_edge_idle",
  "feelingSource": "resort_resolver",
  "feelingNormalized": 9,
  "renderedFeeling": "it got nervous",
  "importance": 55,
  "mapId": "beach_01",
  "resolverInputs": {
    "activityId": "pool_edge_idle",
    "primaryType": "Fire",
    "secondaryType": null,
    "nature": "Careful",
    "mapTags": ["pool", "water"]
  },
  "displayHint": "It seems uneasy this close to the water."
}

blocks.caption: Treasured photo (feeling may be nostalgic)
blocks.body:
{
  "registryId": "mem_000310",
  "pkrid": "pkr_000123",
  "family": "resort",
  "memoryType": "received_photo",
  "feelingSource": "resort_resolver",
  "feelingNormalized": 15,
  "renderedFeeling": "it felt nostalgic",
  "participants": ["pkr_000123", "player"],
  "photoId": "photo_204",
  "linkedRegistryId": "mem_000482",
  "importance": 95,
  "displayHint": "It is holding a photo from the day it played by the beach ball."
}

sections.title: Game-context and silent cache
sections.summary: Cartridge deltas plus surprise snapshots when cache misses.

blocks.body: While a Pokémon remains in an tracked game, record **game-context** tokens when we detect meaningful changes on read or sync:

blocks.html:
memoryTypeTrigger
evolved_in_gameSpecies stage changed in source game
leveled_up_in_gameLevel increased (optional threshold, e.g. every 10 levels)
learned_moveNew move in moveset
ribbon_addedNew ribbon flag
friendship_changedFriendship crossed band (low, mid, high)
beat_gameChampion or equivalent cleared in source game
badge_milestoneBadge count crossed configured threshold

blocks.caption: Evolution context memory
blocks.body:
{
  "memoryId": "mem_000088",
  "pkrid": "pkr_000123",
  "family": "game_context",
  "memoryType": "evolved_in_game",
  "gameId": "emerald",
  "gameContext": {
    "locationLabel": "Route 119",
    "badgeCount": 4,
    "speciesBefore": "grovyle",
    "speciesAfter": "sceptile"
  },
  "importance": 80,
  "displayHint": "It evolved into Sceptile on Route 119 in Emerald.",
  "createdAt": 1234500000.0
}

When the player opens **Pokémon Resort**, run a **silent cache check** against the last stored cartridge fingerprints for each linked game. No UI unless the player opts into transfer tools.

On **cache miss** (party, location, badges, or progress changed since last snapshot), write a **cartridge context snapshot** for each Pokémon in the party at that moment, plus player-level fields (badges, champion flag, map label, time block if known).

blocks.title: Silent cache check
blocks.caption: Miss writes snapshot; hit does nothing
blocks.source:
flowchart TD
    OPEN["Player opens Resort"] --> CHECK["Compare party and progress fingerprint"]
    CHECK -->|cache hit| DONE["No new snapshot"]
    CHECK -->|cache miss| SNAP["Write cartridge_context per party pkrid"]
    SNAP --> STORE["PokemonMemoryRegistry"]
    STORE --> LATER["Days later Pokemon spawns on island"]
    LATER --> FLAVOR["Surprise displayHint on interact"]

blocks.caption: Cache-miss snapshot (Lavender Town before badge 6)
blocks.body:
{
  "snapshotId": "snap_00941",
  "pkrid": "pkr_000025",
  "family": "game_context",
  "memoryType": "cartridge_snapshot",
  "gameId": "firered",
  "capturedAt": 1234567890.0,
  "gameContext": {
    "playerLocationLabel": "Lavender Town",
    "badgeCount": 5,
    "championCleared": false,
    "partyIndex": 0,
    "speciesName": "Pikachu",
    "level": 24
  },
  "importance": 75,
  "feelingSource": null,
  "displayHint": "It was with you in Lavender Town before the sixth badge."
}

**Surprise rule:** do not spoil snapshots in menus the same day unless the player interacts with that Pokémon. Prefer subtle emote or inspect text on first meeting after intake.

Fingerprint inputs: `gameId`, `partyPkrids` order, per-Pokémon level/species/moves hash, `badgeCount`, `championFlag`, `mapLabel` or `locationId`, optional `playTimeMinutes`.

sections.title: Storage and surfacing
sections.summary: Registry API, importance, and NPC AI hook.

blocks.title: Read path at resort
blocks.caption: Live PokemonActor never owns canonical memories
blocks.source:
flowchart LR
    REG["PokemonMemoryRegistry"] -->|top by importance| FLAVOR["Interact displayHint"]
    REG -->|memoryType tags| SCORE["NPC AI memoryScore"]
    REG -->|treasured photo| OVERLAY["Held photo sprite"]
    ACT["PokemonActor runtime"] -.->|read only| REG

blocks.html:
Acceptance criteria

  • Cartridge memories store feelingRaw + bitmask validation; never re-derive from Nature later
  • Gen 6 (0 to 23) and Gen 8 (1 to 24) export rules handled
  • Link Trade memory 4 / variable 0 special case implemented
  • Resort memories use ResortFeelingResolver (activity + type + Nature + map)
  • Transfer log and resort events share resolver; imported hops keep cartridge bytes
  • game_context snapshots use displayHint without faking cartridge feeling
  • Silent cache miss writes party snapshot without blocking resort load
  • No raw event log; dedupe and cap per pkrid

items.label: Overworld NPC AI (memoryScore and activity tokens)
